video:video.video:title: Vietnamese police rescue hundreds of cats from being eaten with bust of major animal theft ring
video:video.video:description: More than 40 of the rescued cats were reunited with their owners following the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions that they were found in, the groups said.

video:video.video:title: 1 person killed after small plane crashes on Texas highway
video:video.video:description: A business jet with six people on board crashed on a highway in Laredo, Texas, and caught fire Tuesday night, authorities said, killing one person and causing chaos as people left their vehicles to frantically try to smash the cockpit window and free those inside.

video:video.video:title: Surprisingly smooth G7 summit appears to have given positive lift to ties between US and allies
video:video.video:description: Leaders at the Group of Seven summit on Wednesday threw their support behind U.S. President Donald Trump's tentative agreement with Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire — even though he's offered scant specificity about how that would be implemented.

video:video.video:title: How to protect yourself during tick season
video:video.video:description: Tick bites typically spike in May. Here's how to dress and protect yourself during tick season. (AP video: Mary Conlon and Shelby Lum)

video:video.video:title: Police rescue hundreds of cats from being eaten in Vietnam with bust of major animal theft ring
video:video.video:description: Police in Vietnam seized more than 400 cats in a major bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports.

video:video.video:title: Self Esteem on phones vs faces at gigs
video:video.video:description: Self Esteem says phones at gigs aren’t a problem for her, as she becomes the first artist to open new London concert venue, British Airways Arc.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's 'the boss' on arrival for first meeting of final day at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump told other G7 leaders ″I’m the boss’’. That was his opening phrase as he walked in — late — to the first session of the day at the G7 summit.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's 'the boss' on arrival for first meeting of final day at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump told other G7 leaders "I’m the boss." That was his opening phrase as he walked in to the first session of the day at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump again defends Pulte as acting DNI
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump told reporters that Bill Pulte, who is poised to take over as acting director of national intelligence on Friday, will be in the job "as long as it takes to get everybody else approved."

video:video.video:title: Ghanaian community in Canada celebrates ahead of World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Members of the Ghanaian community in Toronto gathered and celebrated at a hotel downtown to welcome the arrival of the Ghanaian national soccer team, the Black Stars, ahead of their debut World Cup match against Panama on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Schumer slams Trump attempt to delay Clayton's nomination for intelligence director
video:video.video:description: In remarks on the Senate floor, Minority Leader Sen. Chuck Schumer, said Trump’s social media post made it “crystal clear that he is the one standing in the way” of reauthorizing a key surveillance program.

video:video.video:title: Tourists disappointed over closure of Versailles Palace amid preparations for Trump-Macron dinner
video:video.video:description: Hours before US President Donald Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron were scheduled to meet for dinner at the Palace of Versailles tourists unaware of the event were turned away at its closed gates, unable to visit.

video:video.video:title: Smooth G7 summit appears to have given positive lift to ties between US and allies
video:video.video:description: Leaders at the Group of Seven summit on Wednesday threw their support behind U.S. President Donald Trump’s tentative agreement with Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire — even though he’s offered scant specificity about how that would be implemented.

video:video.video:title: Stockholm opens public sauna, aiming to make tradition more accessible
video:video.video:description: Stockholm has opened its first public sauna with the aim of making the traditional experience more accessible.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, June 17th: Plane crashes onto highway in Texas; Trump at G7 summit; Iran says interim deal with U.S. requires Israel to pull troops from Lebanon; Georgia Republicans nominate Trump pick for Senate, but not Governor.

video:video.video:title: Coastal development project linked to Trump's son-in-law faces growing opposition in Albania
video:video.video:description: A massive coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S. President Donald Trump, is facing growing opposition in Albania where Saturday marked the 14th consecutive day of protests in the capital, Tirana.

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem cements Hollywood legacy
video:video.video:description: Academy Award-winner Javier Bardem thanks his wife Penélope Cruz as he creates prints of his hands, feet - and nose - at the TCL Chinese Theatre. (June 17)

video:video.video:title: Archaeologists uncover pieces of history from the Battle of Bunker Hill
video:video.video:description: On the same hill where bakers, butchers and candlemakers battled the most powerful army in the world, veterans of a new generation went on their knees in the dirt – searching for artifacts from the conflict 251 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Huge fire burns at Moscow oil refinery after second Ukrainian attack in a week
video:video.video:description: Ukraine hit a major Moscow oil refinery for a second time in a week and disrupted commercial flights at Moscow airports in one of its biggest drone attacks since the start of the conflict more than four years ago, Russian officials said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: High winds and wildfire trigger evacuations in eastern Washington state
video:video.video:description: High winds drove a wildfire into a Spokane neighborhood, forcing the evacuation of about 1,200 people and potentially damaging or destroying up to 15 structures, according to fire officials.

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann to be sentenced in New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: Sentencing for a Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer in the deaths of eight women is expected Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump unsure whether Iran deal will be signed Friday: 'You never know'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump expressed uncertainty Wednesday about whether his much-touted US-Iran agreement will be signed Friday, cautioning, “You never know with deals.”

video:video.video:title: 'Death Stranding' film to diverge from game's story, director says
video:video.video:description: Michael Sarnoski says he's making good progress on "Death Stranding," the film based on the popular video game. (June 17)

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann gets life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: Heuermann pleaded guilty to charges that he murdered seven women and admitted to killing an eighth victim, though he was never charged in her death.

video:video.video:title: Hugh Jackman is gearing up to play Long John Silver in 'Treasure Island'
video:video.video:description: Hugh Jackman has signed on to play pirate Long John Silver in a new adaptation of "Treasure Island," directed by Ridley Scott. (June 17)

video:video.video:title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announces millions in funding to help with addiction recovery
video:video.video:description: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced millions in new funding to assist addiction recovery and mental illness services (AP video by Mike Householder)
video:video.video:tag: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

video:video.video:title: Israeli settlers burn and deface mosques in overnight attack in occupied West Bank
video:video.video:description: Israeli settlers vandalized two mosques in the Israeli-occupied West Bank overnight Tuesday, in what Palestinians say is part of an escalating wave of attacks deep in their villages and towns.

video:video.video:title: As G7 summit closes, Trump takes a new attitude toward allies with mixed messages on China
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump, not known to be a fan of international gatherings of world leaders, arrived at the Group of Seven summit on an unusually high note.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire that destroyed homes in Spokane, Washington
video:video.video:description: High winds drove a wildfire into a Spokane neighborhood, forcing the evacuation of about 1,200 people and potentially damaging or destroying up to 15 structures, according to fire officials.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged, Chairman Warsh holds first news conference
video:video.video:description: The Federal Reserve kept its key rate unchanged Wednesday yet half the central bank’s policymakers said they could support a rate hike later this year.

video:video.video:title: Victims' relatives recall confronting Gilgo Beach serial killer at sentencing
video:video.video:description: Liliana Waterman, daughter of victim Megan Waterman, asked members of the media after sentencing to stop using Heurmann’s face going forward and to instead continue honoring the victims.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles warehouse fire in Boyle Heights prompts shelter-in-place orders due to hazardous air
video:video.video:description: A fire at a massive warehouse blackened the skies near downtown Los Angeles on Wednesday afternoon, prompting shelter-in-place orders in the area due to the risk of hazardous air.

video:video.video:title: Asian shares shrug off Wall St blues following signing of US-Iran deal on ending the war
video:video.video:description: Shares have surged in Asia, with benchmarks in Japan and South Korea setting fresh records, after the U.S. and Iran signed their initial agreement aimed at ending the war.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles warehouse fire engulfs the city in toxic smoke
video:video.video:description: A fire at a massive warehouse blackened the skies near downtown Los Angeles on Wednesday afternoon, prompting shelter-in-place orders in the area due to the risk of hazardous air.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana residents fill sandbags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur
video:video.video:description: Louisiana residents are filling bags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur, the first tropical cyclone of the season which is expected to produce 20 inches (50 centimeters) of rainfall in some areas.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he 'might' stay in Europe for Iran deal signing, quips Vance gets the blame if it fails
video:video.video:description: Speaking at a news conference as the G7 comes to a close, President Donald Trump says he "might" stick around in Europe for the signing of his Iran deal in Switzerland and jokes that if the agreement is a failure, he’s blaming his Vice President JD Vance.

video:video.video:title: Luigi Mangione will assert psychiatric defense in murder case in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
video:video.video:description: Luigi Mangione will assert a psychiatric defense at his state murder trial in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

video:video.video:title: Senate Dems slam Trump as president makes demands and delays his own national intelligence nominee
video:video.video:description: Senate Democrats slammed President Donald Trump after the president derailed the confirmation process of his own nominee to head the nation’s intelligence agencies.

video:video.video:title: US stocks sink on worries of possible hike to interest rates this year by the Federal Reserve
video:video.video:description: Higher rates can tap the brakes on inflation, but they also slow the economy and hurt prices for investments.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 17th: Trump departs for Versailles after G7 summit; Putin hosts leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations; Gilgo Beach serial killer sentenced; Robotics on display in Paris at VivaTech 2026.

video:video.video:title: Local officials praise 'Samaritans' who helped in deadly small jet crash in Texas
video:video.video:description: The cause of the crash was not immediately known. Laredo Police Chief Miguel Rodriguez said investigators working to reconstruct the crash were at the crash site Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Relatives mourn two Palestinians killed in Israeli strike on Khan Younis in southern Gaza
video:video.video:description: An Israeli strike on Wednesday killed at least two Palestinians in southern Gaza and wounded six others, according to health officials at Nasser hospital, where the casualties were taken.

video:video.video:title: Heat problems are hard for India's textile factories to solve
video:video.video:description: Heat waves make working in India's textile factories, which already generate a lot of heat, even harder.

video:video.video:title: Trump offers sympathy to Lebanon and said a copy of Iran deal had been sent to Israel
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump repeatedly expressed sympathy for Lebanon while criticizing Israel, calling it a source of tension with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

video:video.video:title: Survivors of first Ebola Bundibugyo strain in 2007 share how the town learned from their experience
video:video.video:description: Two decades ago, the first-ever outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola emerged as an unfamiliar and alarming illness.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at Versailles to have dinner with Macron following G7 summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump wrapped up his time with world leaders at the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, and made a stop at the Palace of Versailles outside of Paris for a glitzy dinner with French President Emmanuel Macron and his wife, Brigitte.

video:video.video:title: Children and adults in central Gaza receive psychological support through virtual reality sessions
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in central Gaza were longing for fleeting moments where war and trauma did not exist. Some of them visit the Gaza Medical Technology team for a rare chance to put on virtual reality headsets.

video:video.video:title: AP explains what does the signing of interim peace accord by Iran and US mean
video:video.video:description: An initial agreement to end the war between the United States and Iran calls for Tehran to dilute its stockpile of highly enriched uranium and would waive sanctions on the country, immediately allowing Iran to sell its oil freely, AP explains.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan needs US weapons for self-defense as threat from China grows, diplomat tells AP
video:video.video:description: Taiwan's top diplomat in the U.S. says the island needs American weapons to defend against China's growing threat.

video:video.video:title: In Congo, the quest for male heirs outweighs women’s choices, and their health
video:video.video:description: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo and other parts of Africa, some women are having baby after baby under cultural pressure to produce a male heir, even risking their health.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon chief lashes out at NATO allies and announces review of US forces in Europe
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a six-month Pentagon review of American forces in Europe that will depend on how fast they take responsibility for their own security.

video:video.video:title: Trump's new attitude toward allies buoyed by their praise for Iran deal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump isn't fond of international meetings of world leaders. But he changed his tune at the G7 summit after earning support from his counterparts for a tentative agreement he struck with Iran to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Hugh Jackman enters his pirate era
video:video.video:description: Hugh Jackman has signed on to play pirate Long John Silver in a new adaptation of "Treasure Island," directed by Ridley Scott.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 18
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday June 18th: President Trump and Iran’s president sign memorandum; Russia says Ukraine launched major drone attack on Moscow; Gilgo Beach serial killer sentenced; Luigi Mangione’s lawyers plan psychiatric defense.

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison without parole in New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer was sentenced to life in prison without parole Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: What's it like to cover a World Cup game as an Associated Press video journalist
video:video.video:description: From packing gear to waking up early to drive across state lines for FIFA accreditations, Associated Press video journalist Ty ONeil shows what its like to cover a World Cup game and protests on the sidelines of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: 'This was not easy': Trump signs Iran agreement during dinner in Versailles
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump signed an agreement with Iran on Wednesday that calls for Tehran to dilute its stockpile of highly enriched uranium and waives U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'we signed it in Versailles,' when asked about Iran deal after dinner with Macron
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump told reporters that he signed an agreement with Iran on Wednesday, while leaving a dinner in Versailles, France with French President Emmanuel Macron.

video:video.video:title: Putin hosts leaders of Southeast Asian nations, seeking to boost business ties
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin hosted leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations on Wednesday as Russia seeks to bolster business and other ties with the nations of the regional bloc.

video:video.video:title: How people celebrated midsummer 500 years before Stonehenge was built
video:video.video:description: A prehistoric site that could have been an early prototype for Stonehenge has been discovered by archaeologists in England.

video:video.video:title: French minister in the room when Trump signed US-Iran deal in Versailles says it came as a surprise
video:video.video:description: French Finance Minister, Roland Lescure, who attended a dinner with French President Emmanuel Macron and US President Donald Trump in Versailles on Wednesday said the signing of the agreement with Iran was a total surprised for him and the guests.

video:video.video:title: New Yorkers are set to fete the Knicks with a ticker-tape parade
video:video.video:description: New York is celebrating the Knicks in classic style Thursday, throwing a ticker-tape parade for the team that brought home the NBA championship longed for by generations of fans.

video:video.video:title: Yoshiki on artistry, grief and rebellion | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Yoshiki has spent decades criss-crossing genre boundaries as a composer, classical pianist and leader of the rock band X Japan.

video:video.video:title: Tale of the ticker tape: Why NYC covers champions in paper
video:video.video:description: From Wall Street waste to an iconic New York City honor, the ticker-tape parade tradition began as a spontaneous celebration in 1886.

video:video.video:title: Colin Farrell on filming 'Sugar' before the story was fully formed
video:video.video:description: Colin Farrell says making season two of "Sugar" often felt like "building the plane mid-flight," revealing that scripts were still being fleshed out as filming continued.

video:video.video:title: Vance warns Israeli critics of the US: Don't attack the 'only powerful ally' you have left
video:video.video:description: In a rare rebuke, Vice President JD Vance blasted Israeli officials attacking President Trump over his Iran deal, warning that Trump is “the only head of state in the entire world” currently sympathetic to Israel and urging critics to “wake up” to the country’s reality.

video:video.video:title: Teen dies after being thrown from horse-drawn carriage in New York’s Central Park
video:video.video:description: A teenager died after being thrown to the ground when a Central Park carriage horse bolted away from its driver.

video:video.video:title: Residents return to war-ravaged southern Lebanon with hope and sorrow after the US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: Residents returned Thursday to check their homes in southern Lebanon ‘s coastal city of Tyre — once known as an idyllic summer getaway spot — just a week after Israel issued warnings for all of its residents to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Carín León on World Cup song, country music and Sphere shows | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Carín León says contributing a song to the FIFA World Cup 2026 soundtrack fulfills a lifelong dream for the Mexican superstar and soccer fan.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the deal between Iran and the U.S.
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran signed the agreement to end their war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and bring the two adversaries back to the negotiating table over Tehran’s nuclear program, according to details released by both countries.

video:video.video:title: Native resilience shines through as US marks 250th
video:video.video:description: More than fireworks and apple pie, America’s 250th birthday marks a milestone of resilience for Native Americans.

video:video.video:title: Vance defends unwritten ‘gentlemen’s agreements’ with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance defended the existence of a “gentlemen’s agreements” with Iran about how it handles its uranium stockpiles and nuclear ambitions.

video:video.video:title: New York Knicks celebrate NBA championship, receive keys to the city
video:video.video:description: The New York Knicks celebrated their first NBA championship in 53 years with a ticker-tape parade through Manhattan’s “Canyon of Heroes,” and a ceremony on the steps of City Hall.

video:video.video:title: An ancient oak tree said to have sheltered legendary Robin Hood has died
video:video.video:description: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest, linked to the legend of Robin Hood, is believed to have died. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: Iranians in Tehran react to signing of initial agreement with US
video:video.video:description: Many Tehran residents on Thursday reacted with skepticism to an initial agreement signed by the leaders of Iran and the United States to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment

video:video.video:title: Vance touts Trump's Iran deal as a 'win' that's 'already bearing real fruits'
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance defended President Trump’s Iran deal as a "win” for Americans, citing lower gas prices and renewed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Vance, skeptical of foreign wars, becomes the face of Trump's tentative deal to end war with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance has embraced the role of being the chief defender of the agreement he and President Donald Trump signed with Iran over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Former presidents, celebrities, join Obamas to open presidential museum in Chicago
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama, joined by three former presidents, celebrated the opening of his presidential museum in Chicago.

video:video.video:title: New York Knicks Parade
video:video.video:description: Fans celebrated the New York Knicks’ first title in 53 years with a booming parade through Manhattan’s skyscraper-flanked “Canyon of Heroes.”

video:video.video:title: Knicks victory parade rocks New York as fans fill the streets
video:video.video:description: The New York Knicks celebrated their first NBA championship in 53 years with a ticker-tape parade through Manhattan’s “Canyon of Heroes,” drawing thousands of fans dressed in the team’s blue and orange.

video:video.video:title: Obama: Americans want to ‘turn towards each other again’
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama, joined by three former presidents, celebrated the opening of his presidential center in Chicago in an event that brought together world leaders, A-list celebrities, athletes and other internationally known figures.

video:video.video:title: Cat steals the show during Romeo and Juliet ballet in Turkey
video:video.video:description: A cat stole the spotlight during a performance by the Imperial Russian Ballet Company, casually sauntering onto the stage as dancers performed a scene from Romeo and Juliet in western Turkey last week.

video:video.video:title: Helen Mirren leads 'Happy Birthday' song for King Charles at UK embassy in Rome
video:video.video:description: Dame Helen Mirren wished King Charles a 'Happy Birthday' while accepting inaugural UK-Italy GREAT Award in Rome. (June 18)

video:video.video:title: Colombia prepares to head to the polls during the runoff election on Sunday
video:video.video:description: On Sunday Colombian voters will decide between the status quo and a move to the right. (AP/ Samuel Sotomayor)

video:video.video:title: Stevie Wonder, Bruce Springsteen, Bono kick off the Obama Presidential Center
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama formally opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday with help from music legends and superstar acts, including Stevie Wonder, Bruce Springsteen, Bono, John Legend, Christina Aguilera, Marc Anthony and Eddie Vedder.

video:video.video:title: Macron meets Modi and Lebanon's Salam at VivaTech conference in Paris
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam toured the VivaTech technology fair in Paris on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth announces review of US forces in Europe after perceived lack of support for Iran war
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a six-month Pentagon review of American forces in Europe whose outcome will depend on how fast the Europeans take responsibility for their own security.

video:video.video:title: Putin joins ASEAN leaders for family photo before first talks of joint summit in Russia
video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations gathered on Thursday for a second day of talks in Russia as Moscow seeks to bolster business and other ties with the nations of the regional bloc.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde’s Vozinha becomes an Instagram sensation
video:video.video:description: Cape Verde’s Vozinha becomes an Instagram sensation thanks to his performance against Spain and Brazilian channel CazéTV.

video:video.video:title: How many people did you travel to the FIFA World Cup with?
video:video.video:description: From groups of 3 to 300, these fans share how many people they traveled to the FIFA World Cup with.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu vows Israeli forces will maintain occupied 'security zone' in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday that Israel's military will stay in southern Lebanon, where it has occupied up to 10 kilometers (six miles) from the border.

video:video.video:title: Missing persons posters mimic World Cup stickers in Mexico's host city Guadalajara
video:video.video:description: The faces of Mexico's missing are appearing in a familiar format usually reserved for soccer stars, as families adopt the look of World Cup collectible stickers to keep attention on the country's disappearance crisis.

video:video.video:title: Senior Hezbollah legislator says memorandum with US 'a real victory for Iran'
video:video.video:description: Hassan Fadlallah, a member of Hezbollah’s parliamentary bloc says a proposed U.S.-Iran memorandum reflects a “real victory” for Tehran and includes commitments that protect Lebanon’s sovereignty.

video:video.video:title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Thursday, killing three people, as the Trump administration wages a monthslong campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America.

video:video.video:title: Doctor treating Ebola patients describes challenges health workers face at home and treatment center
video:video.video:description: On the front lines of the Ebola outbreak, healthcare workers are exposed not only to Ebola in health centers but also to rejection by their communities.

video:video.video:title: Labour’s Andy Burnham wins a UK special election, setting up a likely push to oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Labour’s Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Scenes of joy in Mexico City as fans celebrate World Cup win over South Korea
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans filled streets across the capital Thursday night after Mexico edged South Korea 1-0 in a World Cup group-stage match, moving the host nation closer to a place in the knockout round.

video:video.video:title: Labour’s Andy Burnham wins a UK special election, setting up a likely push to oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Labour’s Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Cambodian Supreme Court upholds incitement conviction of opposition politician in charged case
video:video.video:description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court on Friday upheld the incitement conviction of a prominent opposition politician while suspending the remainder of his sentence, keeping him out of prison but unable to practice politics for years.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon in intense fighting as US-Iran talks postponed
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military said Friday its forces struck targets throughout southern Lebanon overnight as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area, threatening the nascent agreement between Iran and the United States to end their war.
